items.title: Public Affair solutions to facilitate the adoption of sustainable fishing methods in the Italian seas
items.abstract: Addresses sustainable ocean exploitation through the UN Agenda 2030 framework, examining Italy's position on sustainable fishing and the measures adopted to tackle by-catches. Uses a top-down approach from European to Italian policy, including a survey of 30 Sicilian fishermen. Proposes a dual communication strategy targeting public awareness and fisherman coordination for effective policy influence.

items.degree: B.Sc in Modern Languages and Cultures
items.title: The condition of women in Islam: the cases of Morocco, Saudi Arabia, and Afghanistan
items.abstract: Analyzes the emergence and development of Islamic feminism across three nations through four chapters: pre-Islamic tribal culture and the Nahda period; Morocco's Pink Revolution and the Mudawwanat family code reforms; the evolution of women's rights in Saudi Arabia; and women's associations operating under fundamentalist pressures in Afghanistan.
